reasoning: The FRED data shows the BB option-adjusted spread hovered around 1.6% - 1.7% in July 2025. However, on Friday, August 1, 2025, it spiked sharply to 1.97%. A web search confirms this was driven by a significant market selloff following a weak US jobs report and President Trump announcing new tariffs, highly reminiscent of the August 2024 market scare. Since August 1 was a Friday, negative macro momentum and panic typically carry over into the following week (as seen with "Black Monday" on August 5, 2024, when the spread peaked at 2.49%). Therefore, the spread is very likely to remain elevated above 1.97% by August 10. By September 2, mean reversion is likely to have taken hold, similar to how spreads retracted within 3-4 weeks after the August 2024 and April 2025 shocks, pulling the probability below 50%. For November and January, the possibility of a genuine economic slowdown competes with mean reversion, so the estimates are pulled increasingly towards maximum uncertainty (0.50).
